title: Resolve PR #30 reviewer feedback
description: Address existing CodeRabbit and reviewer comments on https://github.com/opendatahub-io/agent-eval-harness/pull/30 before pushing new commits.
close_reason: All 6 actionable + 9 nitpick CodeRabbit comments addressed in commit 09f862a, pushed to fork

title: Update devtools evalhub-install skill with deployment learnings
description: The cc-rosa-rhoai evalhub-install command (MR !21) is missing several learnings from actual deployment: MLflow schema init workaround details, ConfigMap namespace/label requirements, resource request sizing for 2-node clusters, etc.
close_reason: Updated evalhub-install (+88 lines: namespace architecture, provider registration, container builds), evalhub-smoke-test (+79 lines: MLflow checks, ConfigMap validation, error patterns), evalhub-uninstall (+29 lines: provider ConfigMap cleanup). Staged in cc-rosa-rhoai repo on feat/evalhub-skills branch, ready for commit.

title: Mount Vertex AI credentials in adapter pod
description: The adapter pod needs CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_PROJECT_ID, CLOUD_ML_REGION, and GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS env vars + mounted credentials.json. Currently secret exists (vertex-ai-credentials) but is not referenced in the provider spec. Provider spec env[] only sets global vars, not per-job.
close_reason: Cannot mount Vertex AI credentials in adapter pod without EvalHub volume mount support (EVALHUB-gaw RFE). Secret vertex-ai-credentials exists in evalhub namespace. Workaround options: (1) bake ANTHROPIC_API_KEY into image env (not recommended), (2) use Kyverno mutating policy to inject secret into benchmark pods matching label selector, (3) wait for EvalHub volume mount RFE. Documented in docs/evalhub-rfes.md.

title: Include score.py in provider container
description: The adapter's judge scoring fails with 'No such file: /app/skills/eval-run/scripts/score.py'. Need to COPY the eval-run scoring scripts into the container image so judges can run inside the EvalHub pod.

title: Verify EvalHub + MLflow integration
description: Verify EvalHub health endpoint, MLflow UI accessibility, and that EvalHub can auto-create MLflow experiments.
close_reason: EvalHub healthy (0.3.0), MLflow accessible via OAuth gateway, both services running. Providers need ConfigMap registration — documented in memories.

title: Deploy EvalHub CR
description: Create EvalHub custom resource in evalhub namespace with PostgreSQL backend, built-in providers (lm_evaluation_harness, garak, guidellm), and MLflow integration.
close_reason: PostgreSQL, MLflow, and EvalHub all running and healthy

title: Deploy PostgreSQL for EvalHub
description: Deploy a simple PostgreSQL instance in the evalhub namespace for EvalHub backend storage. Create DB credentials secret.
close_reason: PostgreSQL, MLflow, and EvalHub all running and healthy

title: Enable TrustyAI + KServe + MLflow in DSC
description: Patch the DataScienceCluster on jeder-evalhub to enable trustyai, kserve (RawDeployment), and mlflowoperator components. Wait for reconciliation.
close_reason: TrustyAI, KServe (RawDeployment), and MLflow operator all running in DSC

title: FrameworkAdapter implementation
description: Create agent_eval/evalhub/adapter.py — the core EvalHub adapter. Downloads dataset from S3, stages workspace, executes skill via ClaudeCodeRunner, runs judges, returns JobResults. 2 unit tests.

title: Results mapper — RunResult to JobResults
description: Create agent_eval/evalhub/results_mapper.py mapping agent-eval-harness results to EvalHub JobResults and EvaluationResult objects. 2 unit tests.

title: S3 dataset handler
description: Create agent_eval/evalhub/s3_dataset.py to download test cases from S3 into local case directories. 2 unit tests.

title: Config translator — eval.yaml to EvalHub provider definition
description: Create agent_eval/evalhub/config_translator.py that translates EvalConfig to EvalHub provider YAML format. 4 unit tests.

title: Add eval-hub-sdk dependency
description: Add eval-hub-sdk[adapter] and boto3 to pyproject.toml optional deps. Verify installation and imports.

title: Trigger CodeRabbit review on PR #30
description: After resolving existing feedback and pushing, trigger a fresh CodeRabbit review to catch issues in the new commits (entrypoint fixes, adapter indentation fix, local dataset support).
close_reason: Triggered CodeRabbit review via gh pr comment

title: File EvalHub RFE: volume mount support in provider spec
description: No way to declare volumes in provider spec or EvalHub CR for custom providers. Config files must be baked into the container image. Need runtime.k8s.volumes and runtime.k8s.volumeMounts.

title: File EvalHub RFE: parameter passthrough to benchmark pods
description: Job submission parameters{} field is stripped and not forwarded to benchmark pod job specs. Custom providers cannot receive runtime configuration. This forces baking all config into the container image.

title: Investigate EvalHub callback propagation
description: Job pod phase=Succeeded but EvalHub API still shows status=pending. The DefaultCallbacks reported completed but EvalHub didn't update. May be a sidecar timing issue or callback format mismatch.
close_reason: Investigated: sidecar init container gets SIGTERM before final callback relay. Adapter correctly reports completed status via DefaultCallbacks, but sidecar shuts down too fast (pod completes in <1s). This is an EvalHub operator timing issue — sidecar needs graceful drain period. Not fixable in adapter code.

title: Update provider/README.md with correct registration steps
description: README still shows simple curl POST for registration. Actual process requires ConfigMap in redhat-ods-applications with 5 TrustyAI labels. Document the full label set and namespace requirement.

title: Update provider.yaml to match built-in format
description: provider/provider.yaml still uses object-style metrics {name:, type:} instead of bare strings. entrypoint field missing. runtime.local.command missing. Must match the format used by garak, lm-evaluation-harness etc.

title: Provider definition and container build
description: Create provider/provider.yaml, provider/Containerfile (UBI9), and provider/entrypoint.py for EvalHub deployment.

title: Integration test — full adapter lifecycle
description: End-to-end test exercising adapter from JobSpec to JobResults with mocked S3 and runner. 1 integration test.

title: Add clean examples to repo
description: Commit evalhub-demo-job.yaml and provider/configmap-template.yaml as working examples. The smoke test config already exists in provider/smoke-test/.
close_reason: Added examples/evalhub-job.yaml. ConfigMap template already at provider/configmap-template.yaml, smoke test at provider/smoke-test/.

title: Add provider/configmap-template.yaml
description: Create a ready-to-apply ConfigMap template with all required TrustyAI labels pre-filled. Users just need to update the image reference.
close_reason: Created provider/configmap-template.yaml with all 5 TrustyAI labels and namespace docs

title: Documentation — CLAUDE.md and provider README
description: Update CLAUDE.md with EvalHub section. Create provider/README.md with build, register, and submit instructions.

value: EvalHub on RHOAI 3.4: deploys via TrustyAI Operator (trustyai.opendatahub.io/v1alpha1 kind: EvalHub). Requires: TrustyAI Managed in DSC, KServe RawDeployment mode, PostgreSQL backend. MLflow is separate DSC component (mlflowoperator). EvalHub connects to MLflow via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var. Docs are on main branch of gitlab.cee.redhat.com project 82936 (openshift-ai-documentation). Both are Developer Preview in 3.4. Goal: create rosa-rhoai skills (evalhub-install, evalhub-smoke-test, evalhub-uninstall) following the llamastack pattern (lls-install, lls-smoke-test, lls-uninstall).

OpenShift internal registry requires an ImageStream to exist BEFORE pushing. Without it, docker push succeeds for layers but fails with 500 on manifest. Fix: oc create imagestream <name> -n <namespace> before pushing. Internal image ref format: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/<namespace>/<name>:<tag>

RHOAI 3.4 pre-release install lessons: 1) Use stable-3.4 channel NOT fast-3.x (fast-3.x resolves to GA 3.3.1). 2) Use rhoai+devops_rhoai_readonly_bot robot token from Bitwarden for quay.io/rhoai access. 3) Don't upgrade from GA — clean install only (OLM gets stuck on CRD ownership during upgrade). 4) olminstall setup.sh switches oc context — must re-login after. 5) Only one OperatorGroup per namespace or OLM won't create install plans.

EvalHub provider registration on RHOAI 3.4: 1) ConfigMaps must be in redhat-ods-applications namespace (NOT the evalhub CR namespace). 2) Labels must include app.kubernetes.io/part-of=trustyai and app.opendatahub.io/trustyai=true in addition to the provider-type/provider-name labels. 3) YAML format must match built-in providers: bare string metrics, runtime.k8s.entrypoint array, runtime.local.command field. 4) The EvalHub CR spec.providers[] controls which providers are ACTIVE — must list all desired providers. 5) Auth requires the evalhub-service SA token with X-Tenant header (not default SA). 6) Pod restart needed after ConfigMap changes.

Harness gap: EvalHub operator mounts an emptyDir at /data in adapter pods, overriding any files baked into the container at that path. Container images must use a different path (e.g., /app/eval-config/) for baked-in config. Also: dataset.path in eval.yaml must be relative (config.py rejects absolute paths), resolved from eval.yaml's parent dir. The adapter had an indentation bug where steps 3-7 were orphaned inside the S3 else branch — local dataset path skipped all execution.

quay.io/rhoai pre-GA image access requires the rhoai+devops_rhoai_readonly_bot robot account token, not personal quay.io credentials. The token is stored in Bitwarden vault (collection: 75f54536-fa36-4ef9-8f1a-b09701646cac, item: e6e1fdde-6601-4e8b-8154-b211005518a1). The personal quay_username (jeremyeder) via app-interface dev.yml role only grants org membership, not access to rhoai-fbc-fragment repo. Save token to /tmp/.env as QUAY_RO_BOT_TOKEN=<base64>, then update ~/.docker/config.json quay.io auth before running pull_secret_workaround.sh.

User preference: keep intermediate/helper pods (data-loader, mlflow-init, etc.) around after use rather than deleting them. They're useful for debugging and don't hurt anything. Update docs and skills to reflect this.

Container images built on Mac (ARM) must use --platform linux/amd64 for ROSA/OpenShift clusters. Without it, the image manifest has no amd64 entry and pods fail with 'no image found in image index for architecture amd64'. Always use: docker build --platform linux/amd64 -f Containerfile -t <tag> .

EvalHub on RHOAI 3.4 deployment notes: 1) Deploy to its own namespace (not redhat-ods-applications) per docs. 2) Built-in providers (lm_evaluation_harness, garak, guidellm) require ConfigMaps with label trustyai.opendatahub.io/evalhub-provider-name — they don't auto-exist. Deploy EvalHub with empty providers[] first to get it running, then register providers via API or ConfigMap. 3) EvalHub connects to MLflow via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var on the CR. 4) EvalHub API requires auth (ServiceAccount token). 5) EvalHub route: evalhub-<namespace>.<apps-domain>. 6) PostgreSQL connection needs ?sslmode=disable for non-SSL.

EvalHub adapter deployment findings (live cluster testing): 1) Job spec mounted at /meta/job.json not /config/job.json. 2) DefaultCallbacks(job_id, benchmark_id) — requires both args. 3) JobSpec.benchmark_id (singular) not .benchmarks (list) — each pod runs one benchmark. 4) eval.yaml NOT auto-mounted — provider must define volumes for eval config and test cases. Need to either: mount PVC with eval.yaml + cases, or use S3 download in the adapter. 5) The stubs.py diverges from real SDK — JobSpec fields, DefaultCallbacks signature need updating.

EvalHub RFE gaps identified during provider integration: 1) PARAMETER PASSTHROUGH: Job submission parameters{} field is not forwarded to benchmark pod job specs — custom providers cannot receive runtime configuration. 2) VOLUME MOUNTS: No way to declare volumes in provider spec or EvalHub CR for custom providers — config files must be baked into the container image. 3) ENV VAR PASSTHROUGH: Provider spec env[] exists but unclear if job-level env vars can be set per-submission. Currently only global env from the CR. These force providers to be self-contained images rather than parameterizable frameworks. File RFEs against EvalHub/TrustyAI operator.

MLflow on RHOAI 3.4 deployment notes: 1) MLflow operator deploys pods to redhat-ods-applications namespace regardless of where CR is created. 2) DB credentials secret must be in redhat-ods-applications (where pods run), not the CR namespace. 3) Fresh PostgreSQL DB needs schema init — run 'mlflow server' briefly against the DB first, THEN the operator's db-migration init container will work. 4) Connection string needs ?sslmode=disable for non-SSL PostgreSQL. 5) MLflow uses backendStoreUriFrom (not backendStoreUri) for secrets. 6) MLflow UI URL is at the Istio gateway: https://rh-ai.<apps-domain>/mlflow
